what_it_does: Acceptance test for the relation-type registry: it verifies that relation types are discovered from files in relation_types/, that adding/removing a file dynamically registers/unregisters a type, that malformed declarations raise loudly, that consumer queries (directed/symmetric/as_records) work, and that every discovered type is reflected in relation_types/AGENTS.md.
observations: Imports RelationTypeRegistry, RelationType, _build_relation_type, RELATION_TYPE_FIELDS, REQUIRED_FIELDS from runtime.relation_types
Defines a global PASS counter incremented by a check() helper
Defines a raises() helper that expects ValueError/TypeError
Discovers seed relation types from relation_types/ and checks ids, dict-like access, directed/symmetric flags, inverse, near/far spaces
Dynamically writes relation_types/acc_tmp_rel.py, rediscovers to prove registration, then removes it and proves un-registration
Runs a battery of fail-loud validation cases: empty id, id≠filename, missing directed, non-bool directed, unknown field, non-dict declaration
Verifies directed(), symmetric(), and as_records() consumer methods against discovered entries, not pinned snapshots
Checks every discovered id appears backtick-wrapped in relation_types/AGENTS.md and that RelationTypeRegistry is named there
standouts: The 'add-a-row = a file' dynamic BAR test: drop a file, discover, remove, rediscover
Extensive fail-loud validation cases for relation-type declarations
Drift-home check tying code registry to AGENTS.md documentation
conventions: Heavy docstring framing the test as an adversary-verified acceptance bar
Numbered sections (1 DISCOVER, 2 DYNAMIC, 3 FAIL LOUD, 4 CONSUMER READS, 5 DRIFT HOME)
Manual test harness using check()/raises() helpers instead of unittest/pytest
Uses sys.path.insert and os.chdir to root the process
concerns: Writes a temporary file directly into the production relation_types/ directory; an interrupted run could leave acc_tmp_rel.py behind
Relies on AGENTS.md containing exact backtick-wrapped ids and the string RelationTypeRegistry
No explicit test framework; assertions are ad-hoc
notes: An acceptance test that treats relation types as a file-discovered registry and verifies discovery, validation, consumer API, and documentation parity.
questions: What is the exact behavior of RelationTypeRegistry.rediscover() versus discover()?
What do the seed relation_types/*.py files contain beyond the properties checked here?
What is the intended meaning of 'Cognition Engine NEWMOD · L3/P1' in this context?
How does _build_relation_type enforce REQUIRED_FIELDS and RELATION_TYPE_FIELDS?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it exercises file-discovered relation-type registry behavior, dynamic add/remove, strict validation, consumer reads, and drift-home reflection.
intention_signals: To prove and enforce that relation types are substrate-level entries discovered from files, with no code edits needed to add a type, while keeping declarations strictly validated and documented.
novelty: Treats adding a relation type as dropping a file and verifies the full lifecycle (register/unregister) plus documentation parity in a single acceptance test.
contribution: Provides executable acceptance criteria for the relation-type registry: dynamic file discovery, strict validation error coverage, consumer query behavior, and a check that registry entries are reflected in AGENTS.md.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test file for a file-discovered relation-type registry, covering dynamic discovery, validation, consumer queries, and documentation drift.
intention_for_embedding: To demonstrate by mechanism that relation types are discovered from files, validated strictly, and kept in sync with AGENTS.md.

symbols.does: Test helper that asserts a condition, increments PASS, and prints ok or FAIL.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) is the core assertion helper used throughout the acceptance test. It requires cond to be truthy, increments a global PASS counter on success, and raises an AssertionError with the label on failure.

symbols.does: Test helper that expects a callable to raise ValueError or TypeError.
symbols.description: raises(label, fn) wraps a callable and checks that it raises ValueError or TypeError. It reports success via check() if an exception is raised, otherwise reports failure, centralizing the fail-loud validation tests.
